title: Petition for a Writ of Certiorari
description: This document is the petition for a writ of certiorari filed in the case. It includes the questions presented, the statement of the case, the reasons for granting the petition, and an appendix with relevant materials.

title: Brief of Republican National Committee, opposing petition
Brief of Respondent Republican National Committee
Maddox v. Board of State Canvassers, 149 P.2d 112 (Mont. 1944)
description: In 1944, both the Republican and Democratic parties of Montana jointly challenged a state law extending the ballot receipt deadline for WWII service members. The Montana Supreme Court struck it down on both state-law and federal grounds, holding that the federal Election Day statute required ballots to be received—not merely cast—by Election Day.

title: Voting Integrity Project, Inc. v. Phil Keisling, Secretary of State of Oregon, 259 F.3d 1169
description: The issue in this case is whether state legislation allowing people to vote by mail over an extended period violates a federal statute requiring that the election shall be held on a particular day.

title: New York State Pistol and Rifle Association v. Bruen, 597 U.S. 1 (2022)
description: New York’s proper-cause requirement violates the Fourteenth Amendment by preventing law-abiding citizens with ordinary self-defense needs from exercising their Second Amendment right to keep and bear arms in public for self-defense.
